notes:
Needs XLiveLess (otherwise you'll get "EFLC: FATAL ERROR"). Also needed to copy over nvapi files (https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/350#issuecomment-419514874) and add "127.0.0.1 tv.rockstargames.com@ to  /etc/hosts.
After all that it runs, but not very well. I get a pretty low FPS and stutters which makes it hard to play, but this is propably because of my ancient graphics card.
title: Grand Theft Auto: Episodes from Liberty City

notes: Download the xlive.dll file https://github.com/AccountOneOff/lutris_files/raw/master/xlive.dll and copy it to ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Grand\ Theft\ Auto\ IV\ Episodes\ from\ Liberty\ City/EFLC/. Install wine-staging and wine-staging-i386 packeges (e.g. with sudo apt install  wine-staging wine-staging-i386), run this 4 commands: cp /opt/wine-staging/lib64/wine/nvapi64.dll.so ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Proton\ 3.16\ Beta/dist/lib64/wine/nvapi64.dll.so, cp /opt/wine-staging/lib64/wine/nvapi64.dll.so ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Proton\ 3.16\ Beta/dist/lib64/wine/nvapi64.dll.so, cp /opt/wine-staging/lib64/wine/nvapi64.dll.so ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Proton\ 3.16\ Beta/dist/lib64/wine/nvapi64.dll.so and cp /opt/wine-staging/lib64/wine/nvapi64.dll.so ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Proton\ 3.16\ Beta/dist/lib64/wine/nvapi64.dll.so. Remember to change Proton\ 3.16\ Beta with your Proton version! Add the line: 127.0.0.1        tv.rockstargames.com into the /etc/hosts file.
title: Grand Theft Auto: Episodes from Liberty City

notes: Needs xliveless, no need for nvapi on AMD gpu, poor performance either with or without. (sub 30fps, resolution made no difference, shadows to off improved framerate but still sub 30)
title: Grand Theft Auto: Episodes from Liberty City

notes: After adding line to /etc/hosts and using xliveless it's working with some graphical glitches. It seems playable.
title: Grand Theft Auto: Episodes from Liberty City

cpu: AMD A10-5800K APU with Radeon HD Graphics
notes:
Just download and install xliveless like GTA VI after that run the game. Download here: https://www.gtavision.com/?section=downloads&site=download&id=2914
My wine setup has the standard wine-recommended installation.
title: Grand Theft Auto: Episodes from Liberty City

notes: Refuses to display anything unless D9VK is enforced. Was unable to get gamepad working. XLiveless (https://github.com/AccountOneOff/lutris_files/raw/master/xlive.dll) is a must. Generally is playable, with a bit of stuttering occurring while shaders are being built: was able to get around 40-45 fps with everything maxed out.
title: Grand Theft Auto: Episodes from Liberty City

notes: As other comments mention, install xliveless and it's ready to go
